aerosol-spray-can.description: Ensure spray cans are empty, and recycle with the lid on.

aluminum-beverage-can.description: Empty and free from food residue. Do NOT recycle alcoholic beverage containers.

aluminum-foil.description: Empty and free from food residue. Ball up aluminum foil.

aluminum-food-tray.description: Empty and free from food residue.

baby-wipes.description: Baby Wipes should be disposed of in the garbage. DO NOT flush them down the toilet.

batteries-car.description: Car batteries (and other lead acid batteries) should be dropped off at a Halton Hazardous Waste Depot.

batteries-cell-phone.description: Cell-phone batteries can be dropped off at a Halton Battery Recycling Depot.

batteries-disposable.description: Disposable batteries can be dropped off at a Halton Battery Recycling Depot.

batteries-lithium-ion.description: Li-Ion batteries can be dropped off at a Halton Battery Recycling Depot.

batteries-rechargeable.description: Rechargeable batteries can be dropped off at a Halton Battery Recycling Depot.

beverage-carton.description: Empty and free from food residue. Keep lids on. Do NOT recycle alcoholic beverage containers.

bread-bag-plastic.description: Empty and free from food residue.

bubble-wrap-plastic.description: Empty and free from food residue.

cables-electronic.description: Electronic cables are electronic waste, and should be dropped off at an appropriate location. Visit the link below for more details.

cardboard-box.description: Should be flattened and no bigger than 60cm x 60cm.

ceramic-dishes.description: Ceramic dishes should be donated, or disposed of in the garbage. Be mindful of shards that can cut the bag open.

cereal-bag-plastic.description: Empty and free from food residue.

chip-bag.description: Empty and free from food residue.

chopsticks-wooden.description: Wooden chopsticks can be composted in Halton.

cigarette-butts.description: Cigarette butts cannot be composted and should be disposed of in the garbage.

cigarettes.description: Cigarettes cannot be composted and should be disposed of in the garbage.

coffee-bag-flexible-plastic.description: Empty and free from food residue.

coffee-creamer-carton.description: Empty and free from food residue. Keep lids on. Do NOT recycle alcoholic beverage containers.

coffee-grounds.description: Coffee grounds are compostable, and should not be washed down the sink. Be kind to your neighbour.

coffee-tin-metal.description: Empty and free from food residue.

compostable-plastic-containers.description: Compostable plastic containers (even if they say they ARE compostable) are NOT accepted by Halton Waste Management and are very difficult to compost.

compostable-plastic-cutlery.description: Compostable plastic cutlery (even if they say they ARE compostable) are NOT accepted by Halton Waste Management and are very difficult to compost.

computers.description: Computers are electronic waste, and should be dropped off at an appropriate location. Visit the link below for more details.

condiment-bottle-plastic.description: Empty and free from food residue. Keep lids on. Do NOT recycle alcoholic beverage containers.

cookie-dough-package.description: Empty and free from food residue. Remove lids.

cookie-tin-metal.description: Empty and free from food residue.

cotton-swabs.description: Cotton swabs should be disposed of in the garbage.

dairy-products.description: Milks, cheeses, yogurts, and other dairy products can be composted.

deli-pouch-flexible-plastic.description: Empty and free from food residue.

dental-floss.description: Dental floss should be disposed of in the garbage.

deodorant-tube.description: Empty and free from residue. Keep lids on.

diapers.description: Diapers can be composted in Toronto.

dish-soap-bottle.description: Empty and free from food residue. Keep lids on. Do NOT recycle alcoholic beverage containers.

dry-cleaning-bag-plastic.description: Empty and free from food residue.

dryer-lint.description: Dryer lint should be disposed of in the garbage.

dryer-sheets.description: Dryer sheets should be disposed of in the garbage.

e-readers.description: E-readers are electronic waste, and should be dropped off at an appropriate location. Visit the link below for more details.

egg-container-plastic.description: Empty and free from food residue. Keep lids on.

egg-shells.description: Eggs (both shells and eggs) can be composted.

egg.description: Eggs (both shells and eggs) can be composted.

face-masks-medical.description: Medical face masks and other personal protective equipment should be disposed of in the garbage.

feathers.description: Feathers can be composted in the Halton region.

feminine-hygiene-products.description: Feminine hygiene products can be composted in Toronto.

flammable-liquids.description: Flammable liquids should be dropped off at a Halton hazardous waste site.

flyer.description: Empty. Separate from plastic bags used to cover items; remove elastic bands.

flyer-bag-plastic.description: Empty and free from food residue.

food-can-metal.description: Empty and free from food residue. Do NOT recycle alcoholic beverage containers.

food-tray-plastic.description: Plastic food trays (salad, bakery items, etc.) should be empty from food residue.

fruit-cores.description: Cores (apple or otherwise) may be frozen, dried, prepared, cooked, or spoiled

fruit-peels.description: Peels (banana, orange, etc.) may be frozen, dried, prepared, cooked, or spoiled

fruit-pits.description: Fruit Pits (peaches, cherries, etc.) may be frozen, dried, prepared, cooked, or spoiled

glass-bottle.description: Empty bottles before recycling. Alcoholic beverage containers are not accepted.

glass-jar.description: Empty and free from food residue. Lids off. Do NOT recycle alcoholic beverage containers.

glue.description: Glue should be dropped off at a Halton hazardous waste site.

grains.description: Both cooked and raw grains are accepted.

gum.description: Gum cannot be composted and should be disposed of in the garbage.

hair.description: Hair (human or pet) can be composted in Halton.

hand-cream-tube.description: Empty and free from residue. Keep lids on.

hand-sanitizer-bottle.description: Empty and free from food and residue. Keep lids on.

hot-beverage-cup.description: Cups like coffee cups have a paper coating that makes them traditionally difficult to recycle, but they are accepted here.

household-cleaner-jug.description: Empty and free from food residue. Keep lids on. Do NOT recycle alcoholic beverage containers.

ice-cream-carton.description: Empty and free from food residue. Remove lids.

ink-toner-cartridges.description: Printer cartridges are electronic waste, and should be dropped off at an appropriate location. Visit the link below for more details.

juice-carton.description: Empty and free from food residue. Keep lids on. Do NOT recycle alcoholic beverage containers.

keyboards-computer.description: Keyboards are electronic waste, and should be dropped off at an appropriate location. Visit the link below for more details.

latex-gloves.description: Latex gloves and other personal protective equipment should be disposed of in the garbage.

laundry-detergent-jug.description: Empty and free from food residue. Keep lids on. Do NOT recycle alcoholic beverage containers.

lightbulbs-flourescent.description: Fluorescent (CFL) bulbs should be dropped off at a Halton hazardous waste site.

lightbulbs-led-incandescent.description: Non-fluorescent light bulbs can go in the garbage

loose-paper.description: Paper can be white or coloured.

make-up-pads.description: Make-up pads should be disposed of in the garbage.

medication.description: Medications can be returned through the Medications & Sharps Return program, or at a Halton Hazardous Waste Site.

medication-bottle.description: Empty and free from food and residue. Keep lids on.

milk-carton.description: Empty and free from food residue. Keep lids on. Do NOT recycle alcoholic beverage containers.

monitors.description: Monitors are electronic waste, and should be dropped off at an appropriate location. Visit the link below for more details.

mop-sheets.description: Mop sheets should be disposed of in the garbage.

motor-oil.description: Motor oil should be dropped off at a Halton hazardous waste site.

mouse-computer.description: Computer mice are electronic waste, and should be dropped off at an appropriate location. Visit the link below for more details.

mouthwash-bottle.description: Empty and free from food residue. Keep lids on. Do NOT recycle alcoholic beverage containers.

muffin-cups-paper.description: Paper muffin cups can be composted. If it has a chemical coating/lining, it should be disposed of in the garbage.

nail-clippings.description: Nail clippings can be composted in Halton region.

napkins-paper.description: Chemical-soiled napkins should go in the garbage

newspaper.description: Empty. Separate from plastic bags used to cover items; remove elastic bands.

newspaper-bag-plastic.description: Empty and free from food residue.

nuts.description: Both nuts and nutshells are accepted.

nutshells.description: Both nuts and nutshells are accepted.

overwrap-beverage-containers.description: Empty and free from food residue.

overwrap-paper-towel-and-toilet-paper.description: Empty and free from food residue.

paint.description: Paints should be dropped off at a Halton hazardous waste site.

paper-bag-greasy.description: Food-soiled paper bags should be disposed of in the garbage.

paper-plate.description: Empty and free from food residue.

paper-plates-greasy.description: Food-soiled paper plates should be disposed of in the garbage in Halton

paper-produce-bag.description: Empty. Remove rope handle from bags.

paper-towels.description: Chemical-soiled paper towels should go in the garbage

pasta.description: Both cooked and raw pasta is accepted.

personal-protective-equipment.description: Health-related PPE should be disposed of in the garbage.

pesticides.description: Pesticides should be dropped off at a Halton hazardous waste site.

pet-cage-bedding.description: Pet cage bedding (for rabbits or the like) should go in the garbage in Halton.

pet-food-bag.description: Empty and free from food residue.

pet-waste.description: Pet waste belongs in the garbage in Halton.

phones.description: Phones are electronic waste, and should be dropped off at an appropriate location. Visit the link below for more details.

pie-plate-aluminum.description: Empty and free from food residue.

pizza-box-greasy.description: Greasy Pizza Boxes should be recycled in Halton.

pizza-box-non-greasy.description: Pizza boxes should be flattened and no bigger than 60cm x 60cm. Greasy ones should be recycled as well here.

plant-pot.description: Empty and free from food and residue.

plastic-beverage-bottle.description: Empty and free from food residue. Keep lids on. Do not recycle alcoholic beverage containers.

plastic-beverage-cup.description: Empty and free from food residue. Keep lids on. Do NOT recycle alcoholic beverage containers.

plastic-gift-bag.description: Empty and free from food residue.

plastic-jar.description: Plastic jars (like those that would hold peanut butter) should be recycled with the lids on.

popsicle-sticks.description: Popsicle sticks should go in the compost.

printers.description: Printers are electronic waste, and should be dropped off at an appropriate location. Visit the link below for more details.

rice.description: Both cooked and raw rice is accepted.

salad-dressing-bottle.description: Empty and free from food residue. Keep lids on. Do NOT recycle alcoholic beverage containers.

sauce-carton.description: Empty and free from food residue. Keep lids on. Do NOT recycle alcoholic beverage containers.

shampoo-bottle.description: Empty and free from food residue. Keep lids on. Do NOT recycle alcoholic beverage containers.

shoe-box.description: Should be flattened and no bigger than 60cm x 60cm.

shredded-paper.description: Place shredded paper in a tied clear plastic bag.

snack-wrapper.description: Empty and free from food residue.

solid-styrofoam-packaging.description: Small styrofoam packing pellets may not be recycled.

soup-carton.description: Empty and free from food residue. Keep lids on. Do NOT recycle alcoholic beverage containers.

spiral-can.description: Empty and free from food residue. Remove lids.

styrofoam-beverage-cup.description: Empty and free from food residue.

styrofoam-bowl.description: Empty and free from food residue.

styrofoam-food-container.description: Empty and free from food residue.

styrofoam-meat-tray.description: Empty and free from food residue. Remove film wrap and absorbent pads from meat trays.

styrofoam-packing-pellets.description: Packing pellets and peanuts can be recycled.

styrofoam-plate.description: Empty and free from food residue.

sugar-carton.description: Empty and free from food residue. Keep lids on. Do NOT recycle alcoholic beverage containers.

tea-tin-metal.description: Empty and free from food residue.

tissues.description: Chemical-soiled tissues should go in the garbage

toothpaste-tube.description: Empty and free from residue. Keep lids on.

toothpicks.description: Toothpicks can be composted in Halton region.

tvs.description: TVs are electronic waste, and should be dropped off at an appropriate location. Visit the link below for more details.

vegetable-peels.description: Peels (carrots, potatoes, etc.) may be frozen, dried, prepared, cooked, or spoiled

vegetable-scraps.description: Vegetable scraps may be frozen, dried, prepared, cooked, or spoiled

vegetables.description: Vegetables may be frozen, dried, prepared, cooked, or spoiled.

wax.description: Wax cannot be composted and should be disposed of in the garbage.

whole-fruits.description: Fruits may be frozen, dried, prepared, cooked, or spoiled.

wine-corks.description: Wine corks are collected at some Halton wineries or should be disposed of in the garbage.

yogurt-container.description: Empty and free from food residue. Keep lids on.
